title: Chapter 326 – Heaven Shaking (5)
content:
It was as though a beast from the Classic of Mountains and Seas were hammering against the mountain peak.

The waves crashing against the cave in relentless layers were ferocious beyond measure. Shockwaves erupted one after another without pause.

The brushstrokes of the Slaughter Sect disciples, who had been ceaselessly murmuring and transcribing sound transmissions, went utterly still.

A dread born from something beyond comprehension began to seep through the underground plaza.

Boom! Boom--! Kwaaaang!

The energy waves carrying intelligence from above winked out one by one. The speed was terrifying.

The semi-transparent ripples that had filled the void like concentric circles on a pond's surface vanished without a trace.

This did not feel like the work of human hands. It was closer to a fragment of calamity itself.

"So he has finally come. The most powerful and greedy peerless swordsman of Liaoning..."

Slaughter Chivalry Kim Jon-hwi muttered as though reciting verse.

Perhaps this was how he found composure amid crisis. It suited the brother of Desolate Divine Hero--a man who always did what needed to be done.

Until that moment.

Jeong Yeon-shin had not once lifted his gaze from the sword marks left by the Murong family head.

The flawlessly smooth traces seemed to pull at one's very soul. Even Jeong Yeon-shin's upper dantian hummed briefly in resonance.

The sword strike itself was a divine skill.

Slaughter Chivalry's earlier words were beginning to make sense now--the Slaughter Sect elders who had stared so long at that sword trajectory that they lost their minds entirely.

An extraordinarily mystical quality radiated from the traces left by the Eight Great Clan Leader who had likely spent his entire life refining a single sword. It was the spirituality said to dwell within divine skills.

'There is no extraneous thought in the cutting plane. I can sense only one thing.'

The mental image that nothing could withstand his cut.

The conviction that his sword strikes would descend like a meteor shower in accordance with heaven's mandate.

They say swordsmen who have reached the highest realm communicate through their blades. The Murong family head's arrogant mental imagery came through without mediation.

It was a mental state befitting a ruler of the martial world--far removed from the humility the current Radiant Demon Wing Leader had cultivated. It conveyed an absolute confidence that bordered on nobility.

Jeong Yeon-shin slowly raised his head.

The dark cave ceiling.

A vibration was traveling along the wall, invisible without eye techniques. Sword power traversing an unknown distance of several zhang. Incredibly refined.

Mountain-Crossing Cattle Strike.

A technique that transmits foot energy to a target regardless of any obstacles in the way.

It is usually deployed through fist techniques, but the peerless master now driving his sword down from dozens of zhang above the cliff seemed to transcend common sense entirely.

The sword strike energy waves transmitted straight downward like an earthquake.

Kuuung--! Kuuung--

After pounding several more times, the sound began to fade. As though a predator searching for prey had moved on to another hunting ground.

Only then did a strange relief wash through the underground plaza.

"We're still safe for now. He doesn't know where we are yet."

Slaughter Chivalry Kim Jon-hwi stamped the ground with casual ease.

"The Heavenly Demon's mystical dharma treasure is concealing our sect's presence. No matter how formidable a peerless master he might be--even if I were to dance like a street ruffian here with my power fully unleashed--he wouldn't sense a single trace of energy. It's just... the annihilation of those who formed our sect's intelligence network. That is what pains me."

His words trailed off with bitterness. There was a sorrow uncharacteristic of an assassin in his tone.

Jeong Yeon-shin simply nodded.

Another dharma treasure. A treasure that wielded the marvels of spell martial arts on its own. The stronger one's martial arts became and the broader one's knowledge grew, the more such mysteries one encountered in the martial world.

'I wonder if it's safe.'

He recalled the pebble of the Valor Fist Lord that he had given to Zhuge Qing-ya before heading to Taiyuan.

A dharma treasure imbued with the refreshing quality unique to the Zhuge family--the quality that had inspired the creation of the Fan Dragon Treading Lotus Technique. The girl who had lost both brother and father had stared at the pebble for a long while before carefully accepting it.

--Thank you for your trust. I'll uncover all its powers... Please take care of yourself, Squad Leader.

That was all. He had heard nothing since.

Jeong Yeon-shin had deliberately given her leeway. He had permitted her to act independently.

With Fan Dragon and Revived Hidden Dragon dead, she had become the Zhuge family's sole remaining direct descendant. Zhuge Qing-ya rightfully bore the duty of shouldering her clan as the future family head.

Her original purpose in joining Radiant Demon Wing as a guest martial artist had been to demonstrate the friendship between the Martial Alliance and Desolate Fortress.

A warning directed at the Thirteen Evil Sects would never take precedence over her family's survival.

'Where could she have gone?'

Whether she had returned to Desolate Fortress with the two senior squad leaders or turned back toward home was unknown.

Either way, he hoped she would find peace. After the matter at hand was settled, they would meet again in some form.

While Squad Leader Jeong Yeon-shin was briefly lost in thought, Hyeon Won-chang had seized Slaughter Chivalry by the collar and led him to a corner of the cave.

He was requesting understanding from Jeong Yeon-shin via sound transmission.

--The final incantation of the Slaughter Sect's secret technique that I mentioned was a lie. I ended up unlocking and closing my restriction with the squad leader's martial arts and my own power.

--You mean you couldn't fully release it?

--Later, later I'll tell you everything. Over a cup of bamboo leaf wine.

Hyeon Won-chang only turned his attention to Slaughter Chivalry after seeing his squad leader nod.

"Brother, there's something I need to discuss with you. It's a matter directly related to your future path."

"Is it urgent? Even with the dharma treasure, our situation hasn't changed. Before the monster wielding the Flowing Star Sword bursts in, I need to move the disciples to a safe house. No one knows how much power these absolute beings are concealing. These monsters with their terrifying strength might carve out the entire cliff without warning."

"It's important. You must hear this before the elders arrive. What those old men have been hiding from you, how they've been deceiving you..."

The rest of the conversation became inaudible. They must have switched to sound transmission.

Only Slaughter Chivalry's irregular breathing hinted at the gravity of what was being said.

Occasionally, the sight of Hyeon Won-chang patting his cousin's shoulder offered glimpses of their shared childhood.

Jeong Yeon-shin watched them and thought: Brother Hyeon still cherishes his blood relatives after all.

"......"

After briefly recalling the Jeong family, he turned his gaze back toward the sword marks left by the Murong family head.

The elongated traces of the sword's trajectory were perfect for focusing his attention. They contained so much--strong sword, swift sword, and even the true energy manipulation method that elevated cutting power to its highest level. With a bit more observation, he could even reverse-engineer the grip technique for holding the sword handle.

The current Radiant Demon Wing Leader had always loved such refined martial arts. It had been that way ever since he escaped the Jeong family courtyard that no one paid attention to, using the Jeong Family Dynamic Technique.

Even when his fellow disciples of Radiant Demon Wing were reuniting with their true families, Jeong Yeon-shin was never particularly lonely. Because he always had a mother who embraced his entire being.

How much time had passed like that?

Dozens of presences spread from a passage on one side of the underground plaza, and familiar voices reached Jeong Yeon-shin's ears.

"You look brilliant. As if I haven't seen you in ten years."

"Squad Leader!"

Two figures separated from the group.

The ruffian with cropped hair who walked with a leisurely swagger was the Hwangbo family's descendant, while the girl who bounded forward with cat-like, nimble footsteps was the true heir of the Desolate Divine lineage.

Lazy Flame Dragon and Shin So-bin had reached their squad leader.

"Your energy has shifted again. As expected from someone still growing."

"Were you alright? Your complexion seems fine... I heard you killed that unfilial Zhuge Clan Leader. I couldn't believe it at first, but then I did. Because it's you, Squad Leader."

Lazy Flame Dragon's sharp, offhand remarks and Shin So-bin's chattering greetings that buried them were all familiar.

A faint smile appeared on Jeong Yeon-shin's lips.

"Hmm?"

Suddenly, Shin So-bin leaned her small face slightly forward.

Was she imitating Baek Mi-ryeo? With her dense eyelashes raised, she looked as though she were trying to play the part of an older sister.

"You must have been quite exhausted. It sounds like it was no ordinary journey through the martial world... I'm afraid of getting scolded by Senior Bewitching Sword Demon and Senior Blue Eye Demon Sword for not properly supporting you. No, I'm personally not feeling good about it. May I check your pulse?"

"The white cat hasn't matured yet."

"What? Who's saying what to whom...?"

"At your age, being in the same generation as our squad leader, your stormy period will come late. Even lying in bed, the heart grows restless at that time. Moreover, with our squad leader's karma being somewhat heavy, those legs will inevitably grow stronger night after night, so the martial world will soon fear the Lotus Nezha's footwork more than his sword..."

"Have you been chewing opium while staying at the Slaughter Sect?"

It had been a long time since he'd told them to stop. Jeong Yeon-shin muttered as he watched their banter.

The reunion of Desolate Fortress's Radiant Demon Wing.

It was boisterous, yet utterly natural.

Even as the old assassins of the Slaughter Sect in their black windbreakers moved with cautious glances at Jeong Yeon-shin, the two subordinates of Radiant Demon Wing studied the muscle lines revealed by their squad leader's clothing and the flow of his energy waves.

The discerning eye of Desolate Fortress masters perceives much in a brief moment. Their gazes were withdrawn with practiced nonchalance only after several breaths.

By then, the conversation between Slaughter Chivalry Kim Jon-hwi and Hyeon Won-chang had concluded.

***

https://discord.gg/XC9DTsTQ9Z

***

"I'll lead the disciples, so Seon-hwi, you go to the hidden storage and bring back a small bronze box. It's very small--just a few scraps of paper inside. There's no map or anything... but I know you've already examined all the escape routes and memorized the geography here. I'm counting on you."

"I am not a Slaughter Sect disciple. I don't want to be involved anymore..."

"What can I do when you're the only one I can trust now?"

Slaughter Chivalry's archaic eloquence resembled Hyeon Won-chang's.

His tone was somewhat cold. He had just learned that the Slaughter Sect's secret technique for recognizing a successor as family head had been completely lost during the chaos at the Heavenly Demon's Tomb.

He trusted his brother. Slaughter Chivalry, with his black hat pulled low, revealed nothing before the dozen or so elders who now approached.

"Hidden storage? Sect Leader, are you planning to entrust our sect's precious treasure to Kim Seon-hwi?"

"An outsider! How could you say such a terrible thing? The test wasn't even completed!"

Slaughter Chivalry did not dignify each remark with a response.

"Did you not sense the Flowing Star Sword's presence? Elders, you should come with me to evacuate the disciples first, and the only direct descendant, Seon-hwi, should follow with the relics of the Heavenly Demon's Tomb. In terms of legitimacy, he could even challenge for the position of family head. How dare you elders call this child an outsider?"

"Listen here, Sect Leader!"

"The peerless swordsman of Liaoning has come to our sect. No matter how I consider it, haste must be our priority. That monster of terrifying power might split the mountain peak at any moment, so we should gather the disciples first and head for the safe house."

A proper assassin's safe house is never just one.

The saying about a cunning rabbit having three burrows is a principle assassins must engrave upon their hearts. Multiple burrows must always be prepared.

"Disciples, this way! Those who have just returned, have the Black Messengers send sound transmissions! We will meet at the southern safe house of Taiyuan Prefecture!"

It was the system of a major faction.

He commanded like a general in the guise of an assassin. Dozens of Slaughter Sect disciples crossed the underground plaza following Slaughter Chivalry's gestures.

Among them, Slaughter Chivalry's presence exerted its authority.

The vast underground cave became a hive of activity.

Off to one side, Lazy Flame Dragon clicked his tongue as he watched them assemble.

"Reminds me of Namgung. An assassin stepping into the light."

"The number one sect in Shanxi has been decided amid the chaos. Right, Squad Leader?"

It was then.

Suddenly.

Boom! Kwaaaang! Kwaaaaaaang--!

A roar raced along the cave ceiling. Stone fragments showered down.

The faces of the Slaughter Sect disciples at the stone desks drained of color. This was incomparable to earlier reports about the movements of the Murong family head and Profound Martial Alliance Leader.

"The Forbidden Gate Formation has just been broken."

"Urgent report. The mechanism formation of the Using Shout Wall appears to have been destroyed."

Their manner of speech had changed. Despite their monotone delivery, a desperate urgency ran beneath every word.

They all seemed to have cultivated their crown acupoints, absorbing the cave's energy waves through their Baihui acupoints as they spoke.

"Black Sword's sound transmission has been cut off. No energy waves can be sensed."

"The Golden Immortal Palace Ringing Formation has been shattered! Everything above has been breached!"

"He's coming!"

Wooooooong--!

In the next instant, a deep echo swallowed every voice. As though a legendary dragon had thrust its head into the underground plaza and roared.

The pressure against the cave walls was immense, as though trying to crush them entirely. An invisible force expanded rapidly in all directions.

Something was coming.

Kwaaaang!... With a deafening explosion, dust erupted. All eight directions were choked with it, and the ground beneath their feet shook like a true earthquake.

Lazy Flame Dragon's judgment was the swiftest.

"Better to get out of the way without making a fuss."

His voice, imbued with scorching yang energy, cut through the storm of shockwaves. Without so much as a glance at the Slaughter Sect, he urged Hyeon Won-chang and Shin So-bin to retreat while looking squarely at Jeong Yeon-shin.

The words were meant for everyone. The fierce gleam in the corners of his eyes shouldered whatever burden might otherwise fall on Jeong Yeon-shin.

"It's like a spider's web. Hide in any of the Slaughter Sect's safe houses."

Dust covered everything. Only the vague outlines of figures could be distinguished.

Sunlight had begun to pour down from above, but not a soul at the scene considered it fortunate.

Ziiiing--

A chilling sword sound scraped the air. It came from within the dust cloud that intermittently obscured the vertical shafts of sunlight.

An extraordinarily sharp quality began to saturate the vast cave. At some point, a figure holding a sword had materialized, standing on air, gazing down upon the gathering below.

"......"

It had manifested.

The peerless swordsman who had long coveted the Heavenly Demon's legacy.

Grayish dust surrounded him like a curtain. His features were invisible. Only his overwhelming presence was unmistakable--a force that seemed to physically constrict one's breath.

"Found you."

A deep voice rang out.

The power imbued in that single phrase swept through the hall like the hazy ascent of a dragon.

No one could respond. The silence only deepened.

They had been placed beneath the feet of Liaoning's foremost figure.

The master of the Murong family.

Star Radiance Great Sword Lord Murong Jung-rak.

A name that needed no introduction. The Eight Clan Leaders are absolute beings of long standing.

The moment one sensed their presence, it was as though one's life thread had been cut. As inevitable as the principles of the martial world itself.

Having encountered him, it was already too late. No one dared break the silence.

Only.

At this moment, a figure silently rose into the air. It ascended slowly toward the shadow of the peerless master who looked down upon the underground plaza.

A back view with the hem of a pitch-black long robe fluttering.

Standing on a long sword blade, both hands hanging at his sides.
